purpose: Score based on WHO the consumer is, WHAT they said, and HOW they behave — not product or channel mechanics.

features.reason: Core mood of the lead (angry/satisfied/urgent/curious) — directly reflects buyer mindset and likelihood to engage
Numeric intensity of sentiment — distinguishes mild vs strong signals of the same category
Blended multi-dimensional sentiment score — more stable predictor than raw score alone
Explicit buying intent (ready_to_buy, evaluating, churning, at_risk) — strongest single feature for consumer scoring
Quality of messages (short/medium/detailed/long) — reflects how seriously the consumer is engaging
Length of conversation summary — longer = deeper, richer conversations indicate serious interest
Whether a key action occurred (demo booked, quote requested) — milestone signals active progression
Speed through the current pipeline stage — fast movers close more often
Past successful deals for this customer/segment — direct historical performance signal
Repeat customers have trust built up — lower friction to close
Average past sentiment — persistent positive or negative history shapes current prediction
Baseline close rate for this lead's industry — normalizes score against sector difficulty
Completeness of lead profile — incomplete profiles indicate low engagement from the consumer side
Origin of the lead (referral, email, whatsapp, web, voice) — referrals close significantly higher than cold leads
Ratio of agent replies to lead messages — reflects how actively the lead is being engaged
Whether docs/brochures were shared — sharing attachments moves the deal into active consideration
